original_reasoning: The Arabic evidence points to the nisba or place-name Surt, and the Greek gloss is likewise a place name Sarta.
Arabic طَنَّاز is a mocker and γελοιοτάταιν means most laughable/ridiculous; both center on ridicule and making others objects of laughter.
ملق=to flatter/be soft/gentle; μαλακός=soft, gentle. Strong semantic match
رسخ is firmness and fixation; Greek join or fasten is a close action-level match.
Arabic is to carry, load, or bear; Greek is to take prisoner. Capturing commonly entails carrying off, so the action-result chain is clear.
Arabic بهاء and Greek beauty converge strongly at the surface semantic level.
Greek means 'right there/on the spot'; Arabic gloss says 'where he was' and also road-junction.

Arabic البرنيق: a type of truffle or sediment; also a clan name. Greek Βερενίκη: Berenice (personal name meaning 'bearer of victory' from Macedonian). The Arabic proper name بِرْنيق for a man of Banu Sa'd is a phonological borrowing of the Greek/Macedonian name. Strong phonological match.
